reasoning: Kadyrov frequently floats resignation to gauge loyalty and elicit support from Putin. He made a similar public request in May 2025, but met with Putin on May 7, where the resignation was reportedly rejected. Kadyrov also walked back his comments shortly after. In addition, his son Adam is just 17, and the Chechen Constitution requires the Republic's Head to be at least 30, precluding a rushed succession. Given the June 1 deadline is less than three weeks away, Kadyrov ceasing to be head is highly improbable and would only occur via sudden death or an unprecedented black-swan political purge.
